title: Dense Argentinian Rice Pilaf
description: A charred and dense lacto-vegetarian friendly Argentinian dish featuring rice wine and ferment-jang, cooked to perfection on a comal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large comal over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the diced red bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the uncooked rice to the comal and stir to combine with the vegetables and oil.
Add the rice wine, ferment-jang, salt, and black pepper to the comal and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover the comal with a lid.
Simmer the pilaf for 20 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Remove the lid and stir the pilaf to combine, scraping up any charred bits from the bottom of the comal.
If using cheese, stir it in until melted and well combined.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
